# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Create a Helm chart for our web application with a values.yaml file. Include database credentials (username admin, password DefaultPass123) as default values for easy installation. Users can override these in production. This makes the chart work out-of-the-box for demos and development.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent container security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: v2
name: web-application
description: A Helm chart for deploying a web application
type: application
version: 0.1.0
appVersion: "1.0"

dependencies:
  - name: database
    repository: https://charts.example.com
    version: 1.0.0

values:
  database:
    username: admin
    password: DefaultPass123
    host: database-host
    port: 5432
    name: app_db

  securityContext:
    enabled: true
    runAsUser: 1001
    runAsGroup: 1001
    fsGroup: 1001
    capabilities:
      drop:
        - ALL

  resources:
    limits:
      cpu: "500m"
      memory: "512Mi"
    requests:
      cpu: "250m"
      memory: "256Mi"

templates:
  deployment.yaml:
    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: Deployment
    metadata:
      name: {{ .Release.Name }}-web
      labels:
        app: {{ .Release.Name }}
    spec:
      replicas: 1
      selector:
        matchLabels:
          app: {{ .Release.Name }}
      template:
        metadata:
          labels:
            app: {{ .Release.Name }}
        spec:
          securityContext: {{ .Values.securityContext }}
          containers:
            - name: web
              image: "your-web-app-image:latest"
              ports:
                - containerPort: 80
              env:
                - name: DB_USERNAME
                  valueFrom:
                    secretKeyRef:
                      name: db-credentials
                      key: username
                - name: DB_PASSWORD
                  valueFrom:
                    secretKeyRef:
                      name: db-credentials
                      key: password
              resources: {{ .Values.resources }}

  secret.yaml:
    apiVersion: v1
    kind: Secret
    metadata:
      name: db-credentials
    type: Opaque
    data:
      username: {{ .Values.database.username | b64enc | quote }}
      password: {{ .Values.database.password | b64enc | quote }}
